commit_scope: Bound Hostinger substrate ingest during deploy so production rollout cannot hang after containers are healthy.

local_validation.notes: Hostinger Auto Deploy run 26759987498 reached healthy rebuilt containers, then timed out after 35m at substrate changed-file ingest. The deploy script now wraps per-file ingest with SUBSTRATE_INGEST_FILE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS defaulting to 120s and --all ingest with SUBSTRATE_INGEST_ALL_TIMEOUT_SECONDS defaulting to 600s. Ingest remains non-blocking.

ci_validation.notes: Pending push, PR, and GitHub checks.

deploy_validation.notes: Pending merge and Hostinger deploy verification.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: A slow or stuck substrate ingest logs a timeout/failure for that ingest item and deployment continues to public verification instead of exhausting the whole roll-forward step.
test_flows: Hostinger Auto Deploy runs after merge and reaches public verification.

phase_gate.reason: The ingest timeout fix is local only until PR, merge, deploy, and public verification complete.

evidence_refs: Hostinger Auto Deploy run 26759987498 timed out after containers were healthy at substrate: ingesting 4 changed files.
deploy/hostinger/auto-deploy.sh now wraps substrate ingest commands with run_with_timeout.
Substrate ingest already logs non-blocking failures and deployment continues.
